# Original Planning Conversation
**Project:** venture-home-scheduler
**Date:** 2026-04-08

> This is the raw brainstorming conversation that produced this project scaffold.
> Use it to recover context, resume planning, or onboard a new contributor.
> Paste it into any AI tool with the prompt: "Read this planning conversation and help me continue building."

---

Ignition: Hey — tell me what's on your mind. What are you trying to solve, or build, or figure out? Start wherever feels natural.

User: I want to build a calendar and scheduling tool  
For residential Solar sales teams at Venture Home

Many players will be doing the scheduling for sales: 
Internal teams, primarily inside sales agents, but also operations and project management
Soon to be customers, self setting appointments for themselves
External partners, scheduling appointments directly to our calendar 

We are in several US states- Maine, New Hampshire, Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, New York, New Jersey, Maryland, Pennsylvania

Most of our appointments are in person, so the scheduling tool needs to account for geography. Even within a given state, not every sales rep can make it to every meeting, so any tool would need to include a mapping function or ability to schedule by ZIP Code of availability. We want to keep it so that sales reps are no more than 90 minute drive from home, and with no more than a 60 minute drive between any two consecutively scheduled appointments. 

We need a mechanism for an appointment to be assigned to more than one person, or appear on the calendar of more than one person. The reason for this is that we often assign senior “virtual closers” a.k.a. design experts to appointments that are being run by a junior consultant.  

We want a visual interface where we can see the appointments that are scheduled to the junior consultants, as well as what is scheduled to the virtual closers, a.k.a. design experts

Structure of outside sales teams: 
NYE- New York, East, includes Long Island and Queens
NYWNew York West- includes Brooklyn, the Bronx, Staten Island, Westchester Rockland County, Putnam, some of Duchess, some of Orange counties in New York 
CT - covers the whole state of Connecticut
MARI- covers all of Massachusetts and Rhode Island
MENH – covers southern half of New Hampshire, and Maine along the coast
NJPA- covers all of New Jersey, and for now, Pennsylvania just Bucks and Montgomery County
MD- Maryland, basically from the DC suburbs up through Baltimore
(these will be the teams having appointments scheduled to them) 

I will attach a full ZIP Code, list of all of our serviceable ZIP Codes

Attach list of our sales rep somewhere where they live, depth sheet. 

Structure of inside sales teams: 
Joseph’s Squad- NYE,NYW, MD, NJ
Ben’s squad- CT,MA,ME,MH,RI 
(these will be the main teams scheduling appointments)

So, not a neat overlap between outside and inside sales teams as far as territory. For calendar reasons, we will focus on the outside sales teams and their schedule.


We would want automated reminders sent to customers via text and email that their upcoming appointments 

We would want reminders sent to the Solar sales consultant and virtual Close a.k.a. design experts as well 

We need an ability to overbook the calendar. So, essentially placeholder spaces where we can schedule extra appointments, even if our available representatives are booked for those slots. Placeholder for each state, region would be best. 

Although less frequent, we do schedule virtual meetings as well. Those invitations should include a Google meet link for the customer and sales representative and design expert a.k.a. closer. 

We would need the following meeting types:
Appointment
Follow up appointment 
Contract signing
Change order
Cancel save appointment 
These different appointments should be different colors on a visual representation

Appointment statuses: 
Scheduled
Confirmed
Completed
Needs reschedule
Rescheduled, new appointment created
Canceled
Disqualified
Appointment statuses should show different colors as well, so perhaps a multi level color option. Three levels would be ideal, that way we can also color code whether an appointment is in person or virtual.

Integration with salesforce is crucial for us. We would need the calendar to be a visual representation of accustom
 Salesforce object. 

We would want the ability to have multiple layers on the calendar. For example, I would want to be able to look at my appointments in New York, my appointments in Connecticut, or my appointments in both at the same time. 

We would want a system so that the individual sales reps would have these appointments on their salesforce and Google calendars as well as alerts to their phone numbers

Everything needs to be synced, so that if changes are made in Google calendar and or salesforce, they are reflected on the other as well

We want for our inside sales agents who are setting high volume of appointments, to be able to simply click somewhere to schedule, in the calendar would handle assigning it to the appropriate resource based on factors such as geography. 

Another factor is ownership of lead. The overwhelming majority of our leads are from paid leads the lead source rollup “paid”. These leads are company appointments, that can go to any sales rep, they are not owned by anyone specifically.

There are a couple of lead sources, get the referral and self gen, where we would want it to assign to the sales rep that owns that lead. 

With “get the referral”, leads should be assigned to the sales rep that owns the opportunity of the person that referred them. 
If said sales representative is no longer with the company, it should go to the “design expert” on the opportunity of the person that referred them. If neither person is active any longer, that can be assigned based on geographic availability

We want a desktop interface that visualizes the full calendar for our inside sales teams that are at computers

We want a great mobile app for our sales reps in the field to see and manage their schedule. They are driving around, and will not always have computer access. 

Meeting times: 9 AM, 11:30AM, 2 PM, 5 PM, and 7 PM during the week. 
We are open on Saturday and Sunday as well, but no 7 PM slot. 
We are closed on New Year’s Day, Memorial Day, Fourth of July, Labor Day, Thanksgiving day, and Christmas Day. We are open all other days of the year.

Pain points we have experienced with other calendar systems: 
No intuitive mapping function
Poor communication between salesforce and Google Cal or Calendly
Messy visual interface 
No easy way to intentionally overbook
Technical issues with salesforce mobile app
Inability to book over certain meetings. We would want a free/busy function, so we could book over certain optional meetings, and not book over other mandatory meetings.

We want booking links for all of our sales, consultants and design experts to have to include in their email signature

We want booking links for the following external partners: 
Greenwatt 
Verse
SunLink
Lo Mano
Remix dynamics

We need a booking link to embed on our website, venturehome.com

We were going to a booking link for our Facebook page

We want to easily be able to create new booking links when needed 

We want to easily be able to add and change sales, consultants, and design experts to the availability



Reminder, in terms of booking, we want to be able to schedule by geographic availability. We also want a function where we can override that, and schedule directly to an individual by putting the lead in their name.
And then As close to “push a button and it sets it properly” as we can be is the dream

In addition to the geographic assignment, we also want a way to weight how many appointments go to each sales rep by their skill level? The goal being that our higher ranked sales reps get assigned more appointments.

First appointment and contract signing, “appointment” “contract signing” should default to 90 minutes. The other sales appointment types should default to 60 minutes

An alert to sales consultants when an appointment of theirs is marked confirmed status

We want a system, where time off requests, after being approved by a manager, will block the sales consultant calendar from availability

Ignition: This is a really compelling problem — you're basically building the operational backbone for a distributed sales organization. I can already see how this becomes way more than just scheduling; it's intelligence about territory optimization, workload balancing, and customer experience.

Let me think bigger picture with you for a moment. You mentioned "push a button and it sets it properly" — what if this system became genuinely predictive? Like, what if it learned that certain zip codes convert better with certain reps, or that back-to-back appointments in specific areas have higher show rates? What would you do with that intelligence?

And here's what really caught my attention: you have all these external partners (Greenwatt, Verse, etc.) who need booking links. That tells me there's potential for this to become a competitive advantage — like, what if your booking experience was so smooth that partners preferred working with VH over other solar companies? What would that version look like?

A few things I'm curious about as we think about the full scope:

**Territory intelligence:** You mentioned 90-minute max drive from home and 60-minute between consecutive appointments. What if the system could suggest optimal daily routes, or flag when someone's schedule is going to create traffic nightmares? Could it learn traffic patterns and seasonal variations?

**The overbooking strategy:** This is fascinating. Are you thinking placeholder slots that automatically get assigned when real appointments come in? Or more like "we can fit 2 extra appointments in this region today if needed"? What's the dream scenario for how overbooking gets managed?

**External partner experience:** When Greenwatt books an appointment, do they see your full availability? Do they need to know which specific rep they're booking with, or just that it's handled? Could there be partner-specific booking flows?

Walk me through what happens today when an inside sales agent needs to schedule something urgent. What's the current workaround, and where does it break down?

User: Genuinly predictive sounds great! I would love to use that intelligence to help scheduling. Yes, great if it becomes a competitive advantage. Suggest routes- great. Flags- excellent. Traffic/seasonal variation sounds great. We do run in major cities where traffic  and drive times can vary greatly. Re overbooking- yes, placeholder slots to hold real appointments, because we get some cancels and want to maximize. Re greenwatt and external partners, they just need to know its handled, not rep specific

Ignition: This is getting really exciting! You're essentially building the nervous system for a distributed solar sales operation. The geographic intelligence piece is what makes this really powerful — not just "can we schedule it" but "should we schedule it here."

Let me think bigger with you on a few angles:

**The predictive intelligence piece:** You mentioned learning which zip codes convert better with which reps. What if this system started tracking appointment outcomes in Salesforce — show rates, conversion to contract, revenue per appointment by rep/territory/time? It could start suggesting optimal scheduling based on historical performance. "This zip code has 30% higher conversion with Sarah than Mike" type insights.

**Route optimization:** You're already thinking about drive times, but what about suggesting entire daily routes? "If you take these 4 appointments in this order, total drive time is 2.1 hours vs 3.4 hours the other way." Could even factor in traffic patterns, weather delays in winter states, etc.

**The overbooking strategy:** This is brilliant for handling cancellations. But what if it got smarter about which appointments to overbook? Like, "historically we get 15% same-day cancellations on rainy Mondays in this territory, so let's automatically add placeholder slots." 

**Partner booking experience:** When Greenwatt books, do they see availability instantly? Do they get different time slots than your internal team? Could you give partners preferential booking windows, or reserve certain premium slots for your highest-value partners?

A couple of questions to help me understand the full scope:

1. **The inside sales flow:** When Joseph's squad needs to "just click and schedule" — are they looking at a big calendar view and clicking on open slots? Or more like "schedule this lead" and the system suggests 3 optimal options?

2. **The Salesforce integration:** You mentioned this being a "visual representation of a custom Salesforce object." Are you thinking the appointments live primarily in Salesforce and this tool is the interface? Or is this the system of record that syncs to Salesforce?

3. **Mobile experience:** For reps in the field — beyond seeing their schedule, do they need to be able to reschedule on the fly? Handle same-day cancellations? What's the most urgent mobile use case?

Ignition: Perfect! Now I can see the full picture. Looking at this depth map with the territories and zip codes, this system becomes even more powerful. A few things jump out immediately:

**Regional imbalances that the system could optimize:** 
- MD only has 7 people but covers a huge territory (DC to Baltimore)
- NYE has 4 people for Long Island/Queens 
- CT has 8 people for one state

The system could get really smart about cross-regional assignments when needed.

**The "Closer Only" dynamics are fascinating:** You've got Boris (100% remote), Max (100% remote), Shane (WV but covering ME/NH), plus Justin Robinson and others marked as closers. So the system needs to handle:
- Physical appointments (consultant only)
- Virtual appointments (closer only) 
- Hybrid appointments (consultant + virtual closer)

**Geographic opportunity:** Looking at the home locations, there are some interesting patterns. Like Claire and Darrid Sharkey both in Norwalk CT (06851) — could the system optimize for shared travel days or back-to-back coverage?

This makes me think bigger about the intelligence layer:

**What if the system tracked performance by pairing?** Like, "appointments with Arturo + Boris have 45% higher close rates than Arturo solo" — and started suggesting optimal consultant/closer combinations?

**Cross-regional opportunity assignment:** What if when MD gets overbooked, the system could intelligently suggest "this Northern VA appointment could go to Josh Rosen in Manhattan since he's closer than Cameron in Arlington"?

**Seasonal territory optimization:** Maine/NH probably has different seasonal patterns than NJ/NY. Could the system learn when to shift resources between regions?

A few tactical questions:

1. **The virtual closer assignment:** When an appointment gets a virtual closer, is that automatic based on the consultant's experience level, or does someone manually decide "this one needs a closer"?

2. **Cross-state coverage:** I see Alastair covers NJ/PA/NY and Fariza covers NJ/PA. Does the system need to handle these overlapping territories smartly?

3. **The "GTR" notation:** I see that next to some closers — what does that mean for scheduling?

Let me also ask about the current pain point: when inside sales needs to schedule something urgent today, what's the manual process they're doing right now?

User: tracking outcomes in saleforce would be great

Ignition: Excellent! So we're building a learning system. The more appointments that flow through, the smarter it gets about optimal assignments.

Let me push this further — if we're tracking outcomes, what else becomes possible?

**Predictive scheduling intelligence:**
- "This zip code + this rep combination has historically had 23% higher close rates"
- "Appointments scheduled for 2 PM on Thursdays in this territory have 40% higher show rates"
- "When weather is below 30°F in Maine, reschedule rate goes up 35%"

**Real-time territory insights:**
- Dashboard showing "NYE region is converting at 18% this month vs 24% historical average — investigate"
- Alerts when a normally high-performing rep's metrics start declining
- Territory heat maps showing conversion rates by zip code over time

**Smart overbooking based on data:**
- "Historically, rainy Tuesday mornings in Bergen County have 25% cancellation rate — auto-add 2 placeholder slots"
- "This rep typically has 1 no-show per week — overbook accordingly"

**ROI optimization:**
- Track cost per lead by source and territory
- Calculate actual drive time vs estimated, factor into true appointment cost
- Identify most profitable rep/territory/time combinations

What level of Salesforce integration are you envisioning? Are you thinking:
1. This tool pushes appointment records to a custom Salesforce object, plus outcome tracking?
2. Full bi-directional sync where changes in either system update the other?
3. This becomes the primary scheduling interface but Salesforce remains system of record for outcomes?

And here's a bigger question: if this system gets really good at predicting optimal assignments, would you want it to eventually auto-schedule some appointments? Like "high-confidence" lead assignments that don't need human review?

User: 1. The virtual closer assignment: When an appointment gets a virtual closer, is that automatic based on the consultant's experience level, or does someone manually decide "this one needs a closer"? -- hiostorically it has been manual

Ignition: Got it - so historically manual, but there's clearly an opportunity to make this smarter. The system could learn patterns about when virtual closers get assigned and start suggesting or even auto-assigning them.

Looking at your depth map, I see clear tiers:
- **Sr. Solar Consultants** (Steven Spector, Laureena Giorgi, etc.) 
- **Solar Consultants** (newer folks like Cole Gimbel, Michael Ficarra)
- **Design Experts/Closers** (Boris, Max, Justin Robinson, etc.)

**What if the system learned assignment rules like:**
- "Solar Consultants (not Sr.) automatically get paired with available virtual closer"
- "High-value opportunities ($50K+ system size) always get a closer"
- "First appointments in new territories get a closer for support"
- "Contract signing appointments always get the closer who was on the original appointment"

**Or based on lead characteristics:**
- "Self-gen and referral leads get extra closer support since they're owned leads"
- "Paid leads over certain income threshold get virtual closer"
- "Previous no-shows or rescheduled appointments get closer backup"

**Performance-based automation:**
- "If consultant's close rate drops below X%, auto-assign closers to next 5 appointments"
- "New reps get closers for first 30 days, then graduate based on performance"

This could start as **suggested assignments** that inside sales can accept/reject, then evolve into auto-assignment for clear-cut cases.

What factors do you think drive the manual decision today? Is it consultant experience, deal size, lead source, gut feel, or something else?

And here's the bigger question: **could the virtual closer assignment become part of the "push button and schedule" dream?** Where the system not only finds the optimal consultant geographically, but also determines if they need closer support?

User: What if the system learned assignment rules: that would be best. All categories mentioned could be helpful. Suggested assignments would be great.  could the virtual closer assignment become part of the "push button and schedule" dream? - yes, eventually

Ignition: Perfect! This is getting really powerful. So we're talking about a system that evolves from "manual everything" to "push button, everything optimized automatically."

**The learning progression could be:**
1. **Phase 1:** Track manual decisions to build training data
2. **Phase 2:** Show suggestions ("Based on similar appointments, consider adding Boris as virtual closer - 34% higher close rate")
3. **Phase 3:** Auto-assign high-confidence scenarios, flag uncertain ones for review
4. **Phase 4:** Full "push button" scheduling with intelligent overrides only

**The rule engine could learn things like:**
- "When Cole Gimbel (Solar Consultant) gets a $60K+ deal, pair with Boris - that combo closes 28% more"
- "Referral leads from existing customers + new consultant = always add closer"
- "First appointments in 19114 (Fariza's Philly territory) + any consultant other than Fariza = suggest Max as virtual support"

**Smart conflict resolution:**
- "Justin Robinson (closer) is already on 3 appointments Tuesday - suggest Shane instead"
- "This zip code typically needs in-person presence, but Boris is virtual-only - flag for manual review"

This makes me think about the **appointment creation flow**. Instead of current manual process, inside sales could just:
1. Click "Schedule Appointment" 
2. System analyzes: zip code, lead source, deal size, consultant availability, closer needs
3. Presents: "Suggested: 2 PM Thursday with Arturo + Boris (virtual) - 92% confidence optimal assignment"
4. One click to confirm, or expand to see alternatives

**What data points would feed the learning engine?** Obviously close rates, but also:
- Show rates by pairing
- Customer satisfaction scores 
- Deal size outcomes
- Time to close
- Upsell rates

Is there anything in your current Salesforce setup that tracks which closer was on which appointment? Or would this system need to create that tracking from scratch?

User: What data points would feed the learning engine? Obviously close rates, but also:
- Completed appointment rate (of x scheduled, how many completed)
- Deal size outcomes
- Time to close
- Gap between 1st appt and 1st scheduled contract signing 
- cancelled deal rate 
Is there anything in your current Salesforce setup that tracks which closer was on which appointment? Or would this system need to create that tracking from scratch? -- Yes, there is a field called "design expert" on opportunities

Ignition: Excellent! So we already have the "Design Expert" field in Salesforce - that's huge for building the training data. The system can immediately start analyzing historical patterns of consultant + design expert combinations against outcomes.

**This opens up some really sophisticated analysis:**
- "Arturo + Boris combo has 15% faster time to close than Arturo + Max"
- "When Kent Sednaoui handles Bedford Hills area (his home zip) with Justin Robinson as closer, completed appointment rate jumps to 94%"  
- "Gap between first appointment and contract signing is 12 days shorter when Alastair (Sr. Consultant) works with any closer vs. solo"

**The "gap between 1st appt and contract signing" metric is particularly smart** - that's measuring sales cycle efficiency, not just final outcomes. Could identify which pairings keep momentum going vs. which ones stall.

**Historical data goldmine:** Since the Design Expert field exists, you could potentially backfill months or years of appointment/outcome data to train the system from day one. How far back does that data go?

**What about tracking appointment outcomes that don't lead to opportunities?** Like appointments that end in "not qualified" or "no interest" - do those get recorded somewhere, or would this system need to create that tracking layer?

**Contract signing appointments:** I noticed that's one of your meeting types. Does the same Design Expert from the original appointment typically handle the contract signing, or does that get reassigned?

The learning potential here is massive. We could literally build the world's smartest solar sales scheduling engine.

User: Historical data goldmine: Since the Design Expert field exists, you could potentially backfill months or years of appointment/outcome data to train the system from day one. How far back does that data go? -- about 2 years. 
Regareding appointments and opportunities-- we consider it an opportunity once an appointment is scheduled. Prior to that iots just a lead. Lead status and all appointment statuses are tracked. 
Does the same Design Expert from the original appointment typically handle the contract signing, or does that get reassigned?-- Same!

Ignition: This is even better than I thought! **Two years of historical data means this system could launch with sophisticated intelligence from day one.** No waiting months to build up training data.

**The lead-to-opportunity flow is perfect:** 
- Lead comes in → gets scheduled → becomes Opportunity with consultant/design expert assigned
- All appointment statuses are tracked
- We can measure the full funnel from "scheduled" → "completed" → "contract signed"

**Design expert continuity is brilliant for tracking:** 
- Does the same pairing that gets the first "completed" appointment also close the deal? 
- Which consultant + design expert combos have the highest "appointment → contract signing" conversion?
- How does performance change when the design expert has to switch between first appointment and contract signing?

**This gives us incredibly rich training data:**
- 2 years of consultant assignments by zip code
- 2 years of design expert pairings and outcomes  
- Complete appointment status progressions
- Lead source performance by territory and rep
- Seasonal patterns by region

**Immediate intelligence possibilities:**
- "In the last 24 months, appointments in 07726 (Alastair's home zip) with Alastair + Boris had 67% contract signing rate vs 34% industry average"
- "Referral leads in CT with Steve Platt + Justin Robinson close in 18 days on average vs 31 days for other combinations"

**Quick clarification:** When you say "all appointment statuses are tracked" - is that in a custom field on the Opportunity, or separate appointment records? Understanding the data structure will help design the optimal integration.

This system could legitimately become a competitive moat. No other solar company would have this level of scheduling intelligence.
